content.problemStatement:
This problem is about sequences of positive integers a1,a2,...,aNa_1,a_2,...,a_Na1​,a2​,...,aN​. A subsequence of a sequence is anything obtained by dropping some of the elements. For example, 3,7,11,33,7,11,33,7,11,3 is a subsequence of 6,3,11,5,7,4,3,11,5,36,3,11,5,7,4,3,11,5,36,3,11,5,7,4,3,11,5,3 , but 3,3,73,3,73,3,7 is not a subsequence of 6,3,11,5,7,4,3,11,5,36,3,11,5,7,4,3,11,5,36,3,11,5,7,4,3,11,5,3 .

A fully dividing sequence is a sequence a1,a2,...,aNa_1,a_2,...,a_Na1​,a2​,...,aN​ where aia_iai​ divides aja_jaj​ whenever i<ji < ji<j. For example, 3,15,60,7203,15,60,7203,15,60,720 is a fully dividing sequence.

Given a sequence of integers your aim is to find the length of the longest fully dividing subsequence of this sequence.

Consider the sequence 2,3,7,8,14,39,145,76,3202,3,7,8,14,39,145,76,3202,3,7,8,14,39,145,76,320

It has a fully dividing sequence of length 333, namely 2,8,3202,8,3202,8,320, but none of length 444 or greater.

Consider the sequence 2,11,16,12,36,60,71,17,29,144,288,129,432,9932,11,16,12,36,60,71,17,29,144,288,129,432,9932,11,16,12,36,60,71,17,29,144,288,129,432,993.

It has two fully dividing subsequences of length 555,


2,11,16,12,36,60,71,17,29,144,288,129,432,9932,11,16,12,36,60,71,17,29,144,288,129,432,9932,11,16,12,36,60,71,17,29,144,288,129,432,993 and
2,11,16,12,36,60,71,17,29,144,288,129,432,9932,11,16,12,36,60,71,17,29,144,288,129,432,9932,11,16,12,36,60,71,17,29,144,288,129,432,993
and none of length 666 or greater.
Input:The first line of input contains a single positive integer NNN indicating the length of the input sequence. Lines 2,...,N+12,...,N+12,...,N+1 contain one integer each. The integer on line i+1i+1i+1 is aia_iai​.

Output:Your output should consist of a single integer indicating the length of the longest fully dividing subsequence of the input sequence.
